claim: Local LLMs (Qwen 3.6 27B, Qwen 3 80B) are now viable for production coding tasks in regulated/IP-sensitive environments
actionable: Evaluate local models for use cases where cloud-based LLMs are forbidden (ITAR, HIPAA, hedge funds, defense)
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Cloud models (Opus 4.7) are still 2-5x faster than local models; use when latency is critical
actionable: For speed-critical coding tasks, use cloud LLMs; for compliance-critical tasks, use local models
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Local models need more specific, detailed prompts than frontier models to produce high-quality code
actionable: When using local models, give more context, break tasks smaller, provide examples
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Local models produce architectural bugs (e.g., routing diamonds through star math) that compile but break at runtime
actionable: Test local model outputs more thoroughly; don't assume passing type checks means correctness
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Knowledge graphs and traditional ML models are still necessary in financial AI; don't use LLMs for mathematical reasoning
actionable: Don't ask LLMs to calculate financial metrics directly; integrate them with specialized tools and models
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: AMD Threadripper + Radeon AI Pro GPU setup enables running 27B models fully on GPU (32GB VRAM)
actionable: For local AI, invest in GPU VRAM; 32GB enables full model inference on a single GPU
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Lower-quality code from local models is acceptable if you can catch it in review; humans must verify output
actionable: Implement human code review for all local model output; don't auto-merge
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Frontier models outperform local models on complex multi-module features (e.g., Rust bookmarks feature)
actionable: For complex architectural features, use frontier models; use local models for simpler, isolated tasks
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Parallel workflow: work on one task while local model works on another; exploit latency by multitasking
actionable: Don't wait for local models; queue multiple tasks and work on other items while they process
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough

claim: Code that compiles but has subtle logical bugs is the hardest to catch; architectural issues compound over time
actionable: For AI-generated code, focus review on architectural coherence, not just type safety
source_title: Local AI Coding is Finally Good Enough
